title: Calls to std::format with a locale should use the L flag
description: Calls to "std::format" with a locale should use the "L" flagAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellsince-c++20unusedWhy is this an issue?More Infostd::formatand other formatting functions have an overload that allows specifying a locale to format the arguments. For instance, to use a.or a,for floating point values, or to spell the months in dates. However, just passing the right locale is not enough.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: When using std::format with a locale, use the L flag in format specifiers for locale-aware formatting.
